summary: Session 1: docs/modules.yaml manifest reader (readModulesManifest, tolerant read, real YAML parse), module/moduleTitle threaded onto SessionSet + raw module key on SessionSetConfig (manifest-validated; unknown/absent/no-manifest degrade to the implicit module), and the fail-loud global set-name uniqueness check per the routed architecture ruling (repo-family + relPath identity from the existing worktree enumeration, one flagged winner row via duplicateNameError, readAllSessionSetsWithDiagnostics envelope, deduped re-arming console.error; the legitimate main+worktree merge is unchanged). 18 new unit tests. Evidence per layer: unit suite 1291 passing locally; Layer 1 pytest 2905 passed/6 skipped locally with the one pre-existing drift-guard failure fixed in this diff (Set-088 doc quoted a banned bigram; reworded); Playwright Layer 3 green on CI for the exact code commit dd5c923 on all three OSes (run 29110488046, fully green incl. drift guards and ceiling gate; local Electron launch is unavailable in the agent shell, reproduced at clean HEAD). Verification: 3 rounds (gpt-5-4). R1/R2 findings all unrated; every actionable part fixed in flight; two dismissals cited in the s1-conventions.md ledger (codeRoots: [] is sanctioned by recommendation 2.4; verification runs pre-close by design). R3 (close-backstop round): 1 Major evidence-integrity finding (suite-green claim vs Layer 3) fixed with the exact-revision green CI citation, 2 Minors (activity-log totalSessions, files_changed completeness) fixed. Per-finding adjudication in s1-conventions.md.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (s1-next-orchestrator-analysis.json, gemini-pro) recommends an Opus-class/high seat for Session 2's cross-surface Explorer module tier (host TS + webview client + ARIA/pixel-compat constraints + Playwright layer), where a visual regression's remediation cost exceeds the seat premium. Supervision points: the ModulePayload protocol contract, the ARIA attributes, and the Layer 3 assertions.
